meta.title: Toggle button group
meta.description: A set of closely related, mutually exclusive or complementary actions that are important enough to be displayed directly in the interface for quick access.

mdx:
## Overview

Button groups can be used several ways, with and without icons, and spanning
from two selections to several.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=2693-2416&m=dev)

[`ToggleButtonGroup` on `react-aria`](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/ToggleButtonGroup.html#togglebuttongroup)

[Relevant W3C Aria pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Size

Toggle Button Groups come in different sizes to fit various layout needs.

#### Medium

This is the default size.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Medium size example (default)" selectedKeys={["md-day"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="md-day"><Icons.ViewDay />Day</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="md-week"><Icons.CalendarViewWeek />Week</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="md-month"><Icons.CalendarViewMonth />Month</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

#### X-Small

This size is for compact placements.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root size="xs" aria-label="X-Small size example" selectedKeys={["xs-day"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="xs-day"><Icons.ViewDay />Day</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="xs-week"><Icons.CalendarViewWeek />Week</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="xs-month"><Icons.CalendarViewMonth />Month</ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

### Visual options

Toggle Button Groups can be configured with labels, icons, or both.

#### Labels and icons

Button groups with icons and labels are used to present a set of related actions
in a compact and visually clear way, where icons provide quick recognition and
labels offer explicit understanding of each option. This combination enhances
user efficiency by making common choices readily available and easily
identifiable.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Labels and icons example" selectedKeys={["li-shipped"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-processing">
      <Icons.RotateRight /> Processing
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-shipped">
      <Icons.LocalShipping /> Shipped
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-cancelled">
      <Icons.Dangerous /> Cancelled
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-refunded">
      <Icons.CurrencyExchange /> Refunded
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

#### Just labels

Button groups with just labels provide explicit understanding of each option.
This approach prioritizes readability and ease of comprehension, making it
suitable for scenarios where icons might be ambiguous or when the actions are
best described through text.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Just labels example" selectedKeys={["label-inactive"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="label-active">
      Active promotions
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="label-inactive">
      Inactive promotions
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="label-draft">
      Draft promotions
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

#### Just icons

Messages potential problems or situations that require user attention to prevent
issues. They require the user to take action to make needed corrections or
confirmation. Use tooltips for accessibility.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Just icons example" selectedKeys={["icon-copy"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="icon-edit" aria-label="Edit view">
        <Icons.Edit />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Edit</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="icon-copy" aria-label="Copy view">
        <Icons.ContentCopy />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Copy</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="icon-delete" aria-label="Delete view">
        <Icons.Delete />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Delete</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

## Overview

Button groups can be used several ways, with and without icons, and spanning
from two selections to several.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=2693-2416&m=dev)

[`ToggleButtonGroup` on `react-aria`](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/ToggleButtonGroup.html#togglebuttongroup)

[Relevant W3C Aria pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/)

#### Just labels

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Labels and icons example" selectedKeys={["li-shipped"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-processing">
        <Icons.RotateRight /> Processing
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Processing Status</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-shipped">
        <Icons.LocalShipping /> Shipped
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Shipped Status</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-cancelled">
        <Icons.Dangerous /> Cancelled
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Cancelled Status</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="li-refunded">
        <Icons.CurrencyExchange /> Refunded
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Refunded Status</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Provide text alternatives for non-text content:** Provide text alternatives
  for any non-text content (e.g., icons within buttons) via `aria-label` on the
  button or surrounding `Tooltip`. Ensure icons have sufficient visual clarity.
- **Don't rely on color alone:** Avoid relying solely on color to indicate the
  selected state. Default styles typically use background color and potentially
  borders or font weight changes.
- **Ensure sufficient contrast:** Ensure sufficient color contrast between
  button text/icons and their background, in both selected and unselected
  states. Ensure sufficient contrast between adjacent buttons.
- **Keyboard navigation:** Make the entire group focusable using the Tab key.
  Allow navigation between buttons within the group using arrow keys (Left/Right
  or Up/Down depending on orientation). Allow selection using the Enter or Space
  key.
- **Clear button labels:** Button labels (text or `aria-label` for icons) should
  clearly describe the action or choice.
- **Focus visible:** Provide a clear and visible focus indicator for the
  currently focused button within the group.
- **No unexpected focus changes:** Ensure that moving focus between buttons does
  not trigger unexpected actions.
- **Provide group label:** Provide a clear `aria-label` for the
  `ToggleButtonGroup.Root` element to describe the purpose of the group to
  assistive technologies.
- **Use appropriate ARIA roles:** Use appropriate ARIA roles (e.g., `radiogroup`
  for single selection, `group` for multiple selection, `radio` or `checkbox`
  for items depending on mode) to ensure compatibility with assistive
  technologies. Nimbus components built on React Aria generally handle this
  automatically.

### Resources

- [W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) - Radio Group](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/radio/)
- [W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) - Toolbar](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Button group guidelines focus on presenting sets of related actions in a clear,
organized, and accessible manner, enabling users to easily understand and choose
between the available options, often emphasizing visual connection and logical
arrangement.

### Best practices

- **Order logically:** Arrange buttons by importance or user flow.
- **Use clear labels:** Keep button text concise and understandable, if using
  icons, utilize tooltip labels.
- **Limit button count:** Avoid overwhelming users with too many options.
- **Space appropriately:** Place button groups close to controlled content.
- **Ensure accessibility:** Make them navigable and understandable by everyone.

### Usage

A button group is best used when you need to present users with a set of closely
related, mutually exclusive (e.g., a user selecting data table views), or
complementary actions that are important enough to be displayed directly in the
interface for quick access. Think of scenarios where users need to make a choice
between a few options or perform a series of connected tasks within a specific
context.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Grouping related actions:** Organize similar tasks together. Use button
  groups to cluster actions that are logically connected, like order processing
  steps or product editing options.
- **Providing mutually exclusive choices:** Offer clear alternatives. When users
  need to select only one option from a set, like filtering by order status or
  choosing a report timeframe.
- **Streamlining workflows:** Enable quick task completion. For frequently
  performed actions on a single item or within a specific context, like managing
  inventory or editing customer details.
- **Conserving screen space:** Present options compactly. When screen real
  estate is limited, button groups offer a space-efficient way to display
  multiple relevant actions.
- **Enhancing visual clarity:** Improve comprehension of options. Visually
  connecting related buttons helps users understand the available choices and
  their relationships.
- **Facilitating efficient navigation:** Offer quick jumps within a section. For
  navigating between closely related views or steps within a specific management
  area.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Do not use a button group as a tab navigation:** There is a specific
  [tab component](#), please use this component instead. There is better
  accessibility support, and consistency with other designs and uses.
- **Unrelated actions:** Don't group unrelated actions together. If buttons
  perform completely different tasks, a button group can be confusing.
- **Overcrowding the UI:** Avoid too many buttons within a group. Too much
  choice confuses users and makes decision making more difficult.
- **Do not use for multi-stepped processes:** Button groups are for single click
  actions, not for guiding users through complex sequences.
- **Avoid if actions aren't logically linked:** If there is no clear
  relationship between the buttons, grouping them may be misleading.

### Selecting views

To allow users to switch between different display views (e.g., grid view, list
view, map view). Make sure that users can easily recognize icons and pair with
hover tooltips.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Do use clear differentiated, and familiar icons for users to be able to
>   toggle options.
> - Do use tooltips to easily explain what a tool is to a user.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Select view" selectedKeys={["view-list"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-grid" aria-label="Grid View">
        <Icons.Apps />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Grid View</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-list" aria-label="List View">
        <Icons.List />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>List View</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
    <Tooltip.Root delay={0} closeDelay={0}>
      <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-map" aria-label="Map View">
        <Icons.ViewWeek />
      </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
      <Tooltip.Content>Week View</Tooltip.Content>
    </Tooltip.Root>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Do not use icons that users will not easily recognize or pairings of icons
>   that seem too visually similar.
> - Always pair icon buttons with tooltip labels for all users.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Select view (Bad Example)" selectedKeys={["view-confusing2"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-confusing1" aria-label="Confusing Icon 1">
      <Icons.Apartment />
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-confusing2" aria-label="Confusing Icon 2">
      <Icons.ProductionQuantityLimits />
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="view-confusing3" aria-label="Confusing Icon 3">
      <Icons.Tour />
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

### Filter information

In data tables or lists, button groups can be used to filter or sort content.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - When using button groups to show filtered lists, pair closely with
>   information to make sure that users know that this interaction shows tagged
>   information instead of changing the state of any selected data. This can
>   sometimes look like an item count included with the button group items.
> - It is not good practice to mix styles in button groupings.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Filter status" selectedKeys={["filter-accepted"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-drafts">
      Drafts (6)
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-accepted">
      Accepted (2)
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-rejected">
      Rejected (10)
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Do not confuse the user with unclear options.
> - Do not use unfamiliar and lengthly labels.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <ToggleButtonGroup.Root aria-label="Filter status (Bad Example)" selectedKeys={["filter-granted"]} colorPalette="primary">
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-compositions">
      Compositions
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-granted">
      Granted
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
    <ToggleButtonGroup.Button id="filter-veto">
      Veto
    </ToggleButtonGroup.Button>
  </ToggleButtonGroup.Root>
);
```
